comment.body: I think what I said here is pretty applicable. Price action was certainly looking pretty distributive over the past 3 days, with a large gap up yesterday (above $115, where a huge call wall was) that was still sold off and retraced $5-$6 to $109… and a gap up that was sold again today.
comment.raw_body: I think what I said here is pretty applicable. comment.body: The question is… who is trying to gap it up? And what does it mean that today’s and yesterday’s gap ups got smacked down? On one side, could be institutions trying to manipulate price to get above gamma resistance. On the other side, seems like institutions could be distributing on up ticks. Really a coin toss here whether this is a bull flag or bearish distribution.

comment.body: 105 looks like the put floor on NVDA going into OPEX. If we don't retake it it could get really bad in the semis. NVDA has a lot of calls expiring this week so the sudden volatility increase will add to more downside (dealer deltas will get longer delta). But on the other side of this you have SPX OTM puts at 5200 expiring which will provide support. So i don't think this is going to be a crazy event for SPX but semis will get whacked
